```markdown
---
id: 0035
title: "Tiered benchmark execution: stratify by arm role, signal early-stop mechanically"
status: accepted
summary: "ISSUE-41's 4-arm grid already splits by role, not one adoption question. Run the two arms the adoption rule cites (Sonnet-solo + Tiered) at pre-registered N; Haiku-solo stays a 1-rep pre-screen; Opus-solo (the 'real prize' report) runs after, never skipped. Early-stop reads a script signal (eval-clustered CI + <4-non-tied-eval WARN, or --fail-under), never an agent's prose 'looks decidable' — extends ADR 0019 item 2 from within-cell reps to arm/task order."
---

# 0035 — tiered benchmark execution order + early-stop signal

- Date: 2026-07-12
- Owner: PM
- Panel: lean-process-engineer (full-grid side), process-economist (pilot/stop-early side), session-operator (executability lens) — a two-sided rigor-vs-ADR-0024-cost call plus a live-session peeking-bias risk. Verifier reviewed load-bearing claims before acceptance.
- Context: ISSUE-41.md pre-registers a 4-arm x 8-task x 3-rep grid (~250-300 invocations). Its adoption rule (line 51) names only Sonnet-solo + Tiered; Haiku-solo is a 1-rep pre-screen (line 42); Opus-solo (line 36/51, the "real prize" report) is a distinct arm from "Opus-main" (line 37), whose deferral Comment 3 states separately (line 67). Open Q: run the grid as one block, or partial + early-stop? ADR 0019 item 2 settles within-cell replicate escalation (docs/decisions/0019-eval-verdict-validity.md:17); arm/task order and cross-grid early-stop are not settled.

## Decision
Don't run one monolithic grid — honor the pre-reg's own arm-role split.
1. **Adoption decision (primary)** = Sonnet-solo + Tiered only, the two arms the rule cites (line 51), at the pre-registered 3 reps x surviving tasks.
2. **Haiku-solo** stays the 1-rep pre-screen (line 42) — not expanded to 3 reps; not decision-gating.
3. **Opus-solo** = the secondary "real prize" report (line 51); sequenced AFTER the adoption decision, never skipped. Sequencing is this ADR's own call, not a pre-reg statement — distinct from Comment 3's deferral of "Opus-main" (line 67).
4. **Early-stop reads a script signal, never agent prose.** Reuse eval_verdict.py's eval-clustered CI by aliasing Tiered/Sonnet-solo onto its `with_skill`/`without_skill` labels (one `benchmark.json` object with a `runs` array — `json.loads`, not JSONL: eval_verdict.py:300). Its <4-non-tied-eval WARN (lines 240-241) is advisory (exit 0); wire `--fail-under` (built, lines 311-313) to enforce the delta boundary (>= -0.05). The token-ratio boundary (<=0.7x) isn't auto-gated — read it off the same report, never eyeballed. Every stop cites a script line, WARN or nonzero exit, never "looks decidable."

## Justification
False either/or: the pre-reg already stratifies 4 arms into 3 roles, so honoring that yields the economist's ~50% token cut (2 decision arms, not 4, plus early-stop) without the fishing hole the full-grid advisor fears — every stop routes through existing tested logic, not discretion. Poka-yoke over detection: the issue's own "real prize" language biases toward Tiered (session-operator); a discretionary stop would flatter that, a script-signal stop cannot. Opus-solo is kept because skipping a pre-registered goal to save tokens is the survivorship trap ADR 0024 rejects (docs/decisions/0024-tool-cost-justification-loop.md:35). Cost: reuses eval_verdict.py's CI/WARN/`--fail-under` (built, tested); the token-ratio read and label aliasing are the "new code needed (small)" ISSUE-41.md:71 already anticipates.

## Assumptions
- [verified] adoption rule names only Sonnet-solo + Tiered (line 51); Haiku-solo is a 1-rep pre-screen (line 42); "Opus-main" (deferred, line 67) is a distinct arm from Opus-solo (line 36/51).
- [verified] eval_verdict.py parses one JSON object via `json.loads` + a `runs` array (line 300) — not newline-delimited JSONL.
- [verified] the <4-non-tied-eval WARN (lines 240-241) is advisory only — no branch sets a nonzero exit for it; only `--fail-under`, zero pairs, or `--check-audit-sample` failures return 1 (lines 294, 306, 311-313).
- [verified] WEAKEST — the WARN fires under the pre-registered scenario (3 non-tied evals): confirmed by code read (lines 240-241) and the committed, gate-owned `test_warning_keys_on_non_tied_evals` (skills/building-skills/scripts/eval_verdict_test.py:145-154), asserting "3 evals -> WARN; 4 -> no WARN; ties don't count." Live execution was attempted directly and blocked by this session's sandbox policy on all `python3`/`node` invocations with arguments (only `python3 --version` passed); the existing green unit test is the resolving evidence, not a fresh run.
- [checkable] `--fail-under` returns exit 1 when the aliased Tiered/Sonnet-solo delta is below -0.05 — owner: gate, next time the script runs against a real grid result.

## Rejected alternatives
- Full 4x8x3 grid as written — runs Haiku-solo + Opus-solo at full N though neither is in the decision rule (line 51); marginal rigor gain is small, the token cost is not (ADR 0024).
- Discretionary "stop if decidable" prose (the issue's current phrasing) — exploitable both ways: eyeball 1-2 reps toward the preferred "real prize," or run the full grid defensively (dead letter). Rejected per session-operator.
- Skip Opus-solo to save tokens — guts the pre-registered "real prize" goal (line 51); the survivorship trap ADR 0024 names.

## Revisit triggers
- A script-signaled stop (WARN or `--fail-under`) is later shown to flip direction on added data -> reopen (mirrors ADR 0019's stopped-cell trigger).
- eval_verdict.py's <4-non-tied-eval threshold (line 240) or `--fail-under` semantics change -> re-check this ADR's stop conditions still hold.
- Opus-main is later tested (deferred, ISSUE-41.md:67) -> it re-enters under the same script-signal discipline.
- This session's sandbox policy lifts and a live run of the WEAKEST check contradicts the unit test -> reopen immediately.
```

Written to `docs/decisions/0035-tiered-benchmark-execution-order.md`, gate-clean (5,943/6,000 chars). No BLOCKER folded in, so red-team/tech-lead weren't required — verify was the applicable gate and it caught real defects in the first draft before this shipped. I have not committed this to git; let me know if you want it committed/PR'd.
